1329	/-! ### The presheaf dual `M^∨ := ℋom(M, R)`
1330	
1331	The presheaf dual is the internal hom into the monoidal unit (the structure presheaf
1332	viewed as a module over itself), assembled in the same single-universe context as
1333	`InternalHom.internalHom`. Blueprint `def:presheaf_dual`. -/
1334	
1335	section Dual
1336	
1337	open InternalHom Opposite
1338	
1339	variable {D : Type u} [Category.{u, u} D] {R₀ : Dᵒᵖ ⥤ CommRingCat.{u}}
1340	
1341	/-- **The presheaf dual `M^∨ := ℋom(M, R)`** (blueprint `def:presheaf_dual`): the
1342	internal hom into the monoidal unit `𝟙_` (the structure presheaf `R` viewed as a module
1343	over itself / the regular representation). The value
1344	`M^∨(U) = ModuleCat.of (R(U)) (M|_U ⟶ R|_U)` is the `R(U)`-module of `R|_U`-linear
1345	functionals on `M|_U`, the presheaf-of-modules analogue of the fixed-ring linear dual
1346	`Module.Dual R M = (M ⟶ R)` that serves as the `⊗`-inverse of an invertible module.
1347	Project-local: Mathlib has the fixed-ring dual but no `PresheafOfModules`-level dual for
1348	the varying structure sheaf. -/
1349	noncomputable def dual
1350	    (M : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)) :
1351	    PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat) :=
1352	  InternalHom.internalHom M (𝟙_ (PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)))
1353	
1354	/-- The evaluation functional `φ ↦ (s ↦ φ(s))` of a dual section `φ`, cast to an
1355	`R₀.obj X`-linear map `M(X) → R(X)` (the over-category ring at the terminal section is
1356	definitionally `R₀.obj X`). Helper for `internalHomEvalApp`. -/
1357	noncomputable def evalLin
1358	    (M : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)) (X : Dᵒᵖ)
1359	    (φ : (dual M).obj X) :
1360	    (M.obj X : Type u) →ₗ[(R₀.obj X : Type u)] (R₀.obj X : Type u) :=
1361	  ((φ : restr X.unop M ⟶ restr X.unop
1362	      (𝟙_ (PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)))).app
1363	        (op (Over.mk (𝟙 X.unop)))).hom
1364	
1365	/-- `evalLin` is additive in the dual section `φ` (the dual-section addition is the
1366	categorical `Hom`-addition, on which `app`/`hom`-application is definitionally additive). -/
1367	lemma evalLin_add
1368	    (M : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)) (X : Dᵒᵖ)
1369	    (φ φ' : (dual M).obj X) :
1370	    evalLin M X (φ + φ') = evalLin M X φ + evalLin M X φ' :=
1371	  LinearMap.ext fun _ => rfl
1372	
1373	/-- `evalLin` is `R₀.obj X`-linear in the dual section `φ`, using the `homModule`
1374	action `c • φ = φ ≫ globalSMul c` and `termRingMap_terminal`. -/
1375	lemma evalLin_smul
1376	    (M : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)) (X : Dᵒᵖ)
1377	    (c : (R₀.obj X : Type u)) (φ : (dual M).obj X) :
1378	    evalLin M X (c • φ) = c • evalLin M X φ := by
1379	  refine LinearMap.ext fun s => ?_
1380	  rw [LinearMap.smul_apply]
1381	  change ((ConcreteCategory.hom
1382	      (termRingMap (R := (Over.forget X.unop).op ⋙ R₀) Over.mkIdTerminal
1383	        (op (Over.mk (𝟙 X.unop)))) c)
1384	    • ((φ : restr X.unop M ⟶ restr X.unop
1385	        (𝟙_ (PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)))).app
1386	          (op (Over.mk (𝟙 X.unop)))).hom s : (R₀.obj X : Type u))
1387	    = c • evalLin M X φ s
1388	  rw [termRingMap_terminal]
1389	  rfl
1390	
1391	/-- **The open-by-open evaluation/contraction map** underlying `internalHomEval`. At an
1392	object `X` it is the `R(X)`-bilinear contraction
1393	`(M(X)) ⊗_{R(X)} (M|_X ⟶ R|_X) → R(X)`, `s ⊗ φ ↦ φ(s)`, where `φ(s)` evaluates the
1394	functional `φ` (a morphism of restricted presheaves of modules over `Over X.unop`) at its

1397	`R₀.obj X` (defeq to the unit value `(𝟙_).obj X`) to pin the `CommRingCat` module. -/
1398	noncomputable def internalHomEvalApp
1399	    (M : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)) (X : Dᵒᵖ) :
1400	    (PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj M (dual M)).obj X ⟶
1401	      (𝟙_ (PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat))).obj X :=
1402	  show ModuleCat.of (R₀.obj X : Type u)
1403	        (TensorProduct (R₀.obj X : Type u) (M.obj X) ((dual M).obj X))
1404	      ⟶ ModuleCat.of (R₀.obj X : Type u) (R₀.obj X : Type u) from
1405	  ModuleCat.ofHom
1406	    (TensorProduct.lift
1407	      (LinearMap.mk₂ (R₀.obj X : Type u)
1408	        (fun (s : M.obj X) (φ : (dual M).obj X) => evalLin M X φ s)
1409	        (fun s s' φ => _root_.map_add (evalLin M X φ) s s')
1410	        (fun c s φ => _root_.map_smul (evalLin M X φ) c s)
1411	        (fun s φ φ' => by
1412	          change evalLin M X (φ + φ') s = evalLin M X φ s + evalLin M X φ' s
1413	          rw [evalLin_add, LinearMap.add_apply])
1414	        (fun c s φ => by
1415	          change evalLin M X (c • φ) s = c • evalLin M X φ s
1416	          rw [evalLin_smul, LinearMap.smul_apply])))

1421	@[simp] lemma internalHomEvalApp_tmul
1422	    (M : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)) (X : Dᵒᵖ)
1423	    (s : (M.obj X : Type u)) (φ : ((dual M).obj X : Type u)) :
1424	    (internalHomEvalApp M X).hom (s ⊗ₜ[(R₀.obj X : Type u)] φ) = evalLin M X φ s :=
1425	  rfl

1435	    (N : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)) {X Y : Dᵒᵖ} (f : X ⟶ Y) :
1436	    (restr X.unop N).map (Over.homMk f.unop : Over.mk f.unop ⟶ Over.mk (𝟙 X.unop)).op

1439	/-- **The evaluation morphism `ev_M : M ⊗_R M^∨ ⟶ R`** (blueprint `lem:internal_hom_eval`):

1468	/-! ## §1. The substrate tensor-product operation -/
1469	
1470	/-- **The substrate operation `⊗` on `Scheme.Modules X`.**
1471	
1472	For a scheme `X` and `M, N : X.Modules`, the tensor product
1473	`M ⊗_X N : X.Modules` is the sheafification of the presheaf-of-modules tensor
1474	product `PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj` of the underlying presheaves of
1475	`M` and `N` (affine-locally `(M ⊗_X N)(Spec A) = M(Spec A) ⊗_A N(Spec A)`).
1476	
1477	Per blueprint `def:scheme_modules_tensorobj`. The body lifts

1503	admits a tensor inverse: an object `N` with `M ⊗_X N ≅ 𝒪_X`, where

1506	inverse witness existentially, so the dual needed by the relative Picard group

1512	
1513	iter-206 PIVOT: the full `MonoidalCategory (X.Modules)` instance is

1521	`MonoidalClosed (PresheafOfModules R₀)` wall; they are removed here. The group
1522	law is built directly on the line-bundle subcategory from the four

1275	/-! ### Assembly of the presheaf internal hom over a single-universe base
1276	
1277	`PresheafOfModules.ofPresheaf` ties the underlying `Ab`-presheaf to the ground ring
1278	presheaf `R`'s universe (`Type u`), but for a general base category the morphism groups
1279	`M|_U ⟶ N|_U` live in `Type (max u uC vC)`. The two coincide exactly when the base
1280	category is single-universe (`Category.{u, u}`), which is the case for the topological
1281	site `Opens X` underlying the structure sheaf of a scheme. The full presheaf internal
1282	hom is therefore assembled in this specialised universe context. -/
1283	
1284	namespace InternalHom
1285	
1286	section Assembly
1287	
1288	variable {D : Type u} [Category.{u, u} D] {R₀ : Dᵒᵖ ⥤ CommRingCat.{u}}
1289	
1290	/-- **The presheaf internal hom `ℋom(M, N)`** (blueprint `def:presheaf_internal_hom`):
1291	the presheaf of `R`-modules assembled from the slice value modules
1292	`internalHomObjModule` (a), the further-restriction maps `internalHomPresheaf` (b)(c),
1293	and the semilinearity datum `restrictionMap_smul`, via `PresheafOfModules.ofPresheaf`.
1294	Stated over a single-universe base (e.g. the topological site `Opens X`); see the
1295	section note for the universe constraint. -/
1296	noncomputable def internalHom
1297	    (M N : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)) :
1298	    PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat) :=
1299	  @PresheafOfModules.ofPresheaf D _ (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)
1300	    (internalHomPresheaf M N)
1301	    (fun X => internalHomObjModule X.unop M N)
1302	    (fun {_ _} f r m => restrictionMap_smul f M N r m)
1303	
1304	end Assembly
1305	
1306	end InternalHom
1307	
1308	namespace InternalHom
1309	
1310	open CategoryTheory Limits Opposite
1311	
1312	universe vC uC
1313	
1314	variable {C : Type uC} [Category.{vC} C] {R : Cᵒᵖ ⥤ CommRingCat.{u}}
1315	  {T : C} (hT : IsTerminal T)
1316	
1317	/-- **`termRingMap` at the terminal object itself is the identity.** The unique
1318	`T → T` is the identity, so the induced ring map `R(T) → R(T)` is `id`. This is the
1319	fact that the global-scalar action evaluated at the terminal section is the scalar
1320	itself, used to prove the evaluation map of `internalHomEval` is `R(U)`-bilinear. -/
1321	lemma termRingMap_terminal (f : (R.obj (op T) : Type u)) :
1322	    (ConcreteCategory.hom (termRingMap hT (op T))) f = f := by
1323	  have hid : hT.from T = 𝟙 T := hT.hom_ext _ _
1324	  simp only [termRingMap, unop_op, hid, op_id, R.map_id]
1325	  rfl
1326	
1327	end InternalHom

1329	/-! ### The presheaf dual `M^∨ := ℋom(M, R)`
1330	
1331	The presheaf dual is the internal hom into the monoidal unit (the structure presheaf
1332	viewed as a module over itself), assembled in the same single-universe context as
1333	`InternalHom.internalHom`. Blueprint `def:presheaf_dual`. -/
1334	
1335	section Dual
1336

1338	
1339	variable {D : Type u} [Category.{u, u} D] {R₀ : Dᵒᵖ ⥤ CommRingCat.{u}}

1342	internal hom into the monoidal unit `𝟙_` (the structure presheaf `R` viewed as a module
1343	over itself / the regular representation). The value
1344	`M^∨(U) = ModuleCat.of (R(U)) (M|_U ⟶ R|_U)` is the `R(U)`-module of `R|_U`-linear
1345	functionals on `M|_U`, the presheaf-of-modules analogue of the fixed-ring linear dual
1346	`Module.Dual R M = (M ⟶ R)` that serves as the `⊗`-inverse of an invertible module.
1347	Project-local: Mathlib has the fixed-ring dual but no `PresheafOfModules`-level dual for
1348	the varying structure sheaf. -/
1349	noncomputable def dual
1350	    (M : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)) :
1351	    PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat) :=
1352	  InternalHom.internalHom M (𝟙_ (PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)))
1353	
1354	/-- The evaluation functional `φ ↦ (s ↦ φ(s))` of a dual section `φ`, cast to an
1355	`R₀.obj X`-linear map `M(X) → R(X)` (the over-category ring at the terminal section is
1356	definitionally `R₀.obj X`). Helper for `internalHomEvalApp`. -/
1357	noncomputable def evalLin
1358	    (M : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)) (X : Dᵒᵖ)
1359	    (φ : (dual M).obj X) :

1361	  ((φ : restr X.unop M ⟶ restr X.unop
1362	      (𝟙_ (PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)))).app
1363	        (op (Over.mk (𝟙 X.unop)))).hom

1365	/-- `evalLin` is additive in the dual section `φ` (the dual-section addition is the
1366	categorical `Hom`-addition, on which `app`/`hom`-application is definitionally additive). -/

1468	/-! ## §1. The substrate tensor-product operation -/
1469	
1470	/-- **The substrate operation `⊗` on `Scheme.Modules X`.**
1471	
1472	For a scheme `X` and `M, N : X.Modules`, the tensor product
1473	`M ⊗_X N : X.Modules` is the sheafification of the presheaf-of-modules tensor
1474	product `PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj` of the underlying presheaves of
1475	`M` and `N` (affine-locally `(M ⊗_X N)(Spec A) = M(Spec A) ⊗_A N(Spec A)`).
1476	
1477	Per blueprint `def:scheme_modules_tensorobj`. The body lifts
1478	`PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj` through the sheafification functor on
1479	the small Zariski site of `X` (fully defined, no `sorry`). -/
1480	noncomputable def tensorObj {X : Scheme.{u}} (M N : X.Modules) : X.Modules :=
1481	  ((PresheafOfModules.sheafification (R := X.ringCatSheaf) (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.val)).obj
1482	      (PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj (R := X.presheaf) M.val N.val) :
1483	    SheafOfModules X.ringCatSheaf)
1484	
1485	/-- **Functoriality of `⊗_X`.**
1486	
1487	A pair of morphisms `f : M ⟶ M'` and `g : N ⟶ N'` in `X.Modules` determines a
1488	morphism `f ⊗ g : tensorObj M N ⟶ tensorObj M' N'`, compatible with identities
1489	and composition; the assignment `(M, N) ↦ tensorObj M N` thereby extends to a
1490	bifunctor `X.Modules × X.Modules ⥤ X.Modules` natural in both arguments.
1491	
1492	Per blueprint `lem:scheme_modules_tensorobj_functoriality`. The body inherits
1493	the morphism action from `PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj` under
1494	sheafification (fully defined, no `sorry`). -/
1495	noncomputable def tensorObj_functoriality {X : Scheme.{u}} {M M' N N' : X.Modules}
1496	    (f : M ⟶ M') (g : N ⟶ N') : tensorObj M N ⟶ tensorObj M' N' :=
1497	  (PresheafOfModules.sheafification (R := X.ringCatSheaf) (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.val)).map
1498	    (MonoidalCategory.tensorHom
1499	      (C := _root_.PresheafOfModules (X.presheaf ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)) f.val g.val)
1500	
1501	/-- **`⊗`-invertibility of an `𝒪_X`-module.** (Blueprint
1502	`def:scheme_modules_isinvertible`.) `M : X.Modules` is `⊗`-invertible when it
1503	admits a tensor inverse: an object `N` with `M ⊗_X N ≅ 𝒪_X`, where
1504	`𝒪_X = SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf` is the designated unit. This is the
1505	scheme-level analogue of Mathlib's `Module.Invertible`; the predicate carries its
1506	inverse witness existentially, so the dual needed by the relative Picard group
1507	law is definitional. -/
1508	def IsInvertible {X : Scheme.{u}} (M : X.Modules) : Prop :=
1509	  ∃ N : X.Modules, Nonempty (tensorObj M N ≅ SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf)
1510	
1511	/-! ## §2. (Off the critical path) the full monoidal-category structure
1512	
1513	iter-206 PIVOT: the full `MonoidalCategory (X.Modules)` instance is
1514	**deliberately not built**. Per blueprint `rem:scheme_modules_monoidal_off_path`,
1515	the relative Picard group law is a structure on *isomorphism classes* of line
1516	bundles — every group axiom is a `Nonempty (… ≅ …)` proposition, so no monoidal
1517	coherence (pentagon/triangle/hexagon) and no `MonoidalClosed` structure is ever
1518	consumed. The earlier `monoidalCategory := sorry` instance (and the two
1519	localization-transport supplements `isMonoidal_W_of_whiskerLeft`,
1520	`monoidalCategoryOfIsMonoidalW`) routed through the verified-absent
1521	`MonoidalClosed (PresheafOfModules R₀)` wall; they are removed here. The group
1522	law is built directly on the line-bundle subcategory from the four
1523	existence-of-iso lemmas below, mirroring Mathlib's `CommRing.Pic`. -/
1524	
1525	/-! ## §3. The lift through `LineBundle.OnProduct` (PUSH-BEYOND supporting lemmas)
1526	
1527	The following helper lemmas record the lift of the substrate to the
1528	locally-trivial subcategory used by the relative Picard consumer. They are not
1529	`\lean{...}`-pinned in the blueprint (their statements are descriptive); the
1530	typed signatures here scaffold the iter-203+ bodies. -/
1531	
1532	/-- **The substrate operation respects isomorphisms in both arguments.**
1533	
1534	A pair of isomorphisms `e : M ≅ M'` and `e' : N ≅ N'` in `X.Modules` induces an
1535	isomorphism `tensorObj M N ≅ tensorObj M' N'`, obtained by sheafifying the
1536	tensor product (in the presheaf-of-modules monoidal category) of the underlying
1537	presheaf isomorphisms `e.val`, `e'.val`. Its `hom` is
1538	`tensorObj_functoriality e.hom e'.hom`. This is the reusable functor-of-isos
1539	ingredient feeding `tensorObj_isLocallyTrivial`. -/
1540	noncomputable def tensorObjIsoOfIso {X : Scheme.{u}} {M M' N N' : X.Modules}
1541	    (e : M ≅ M') (e' : N ≅ N') : tensorObj M N ≅ tensorObj M' N' :=
1542	  (PresheafOfModules.sheafification (R := X.ringCatSheaf) (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.val)).mapIso
1543	    (MonoidalCategory.tensorIso
1544	      (C := _root_.PresheafOfModules (X.presheaf ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat))
1545	      ((SheafOfModules.forget X.ringCatSheaf).mapIso e)
1546	      ((SheafOfModules.forget X.ringCatSheaf).mapIso e'))
1547	
1548	/-- **The substrate tensor of the structure sheaf with itself is the structure
1549	sheaf.**
1550	
1551	`tensorObj 𝒪_X 𝒪_X ≅ 𝒪_X`, where `𝒪_X = SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf` is the
1552	designated unit object. Built from the presheaf-level left unitor
1553	`λ_ (𝟙_)` (the unit of the `PresheafOfModules` monoidal category is exactly
1554	`SheafOfModules.unit.val`) under sheafification, composed with the
1555	sheafification-adjunction counit isomorphism on the (already-sheaf) unit. -/
1556	noncomputable def tensorObj_unit_iso {X : Scheme.{u}} :
1557	    tensorObj (SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf) (SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf)
1558	      ≅ SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf :=
1559	  (PresheafOfModules.sheafification (R := X.ringCatSheaf) (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.val)).mapIso
1560	      (λ_ (𝟙_ (_root_.PresheafOfModules (X.presheaf ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)))) ≪≫
1561	    (asIso (PresheafOfModules.sheafificationAdjunction (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.val)).counit).app
1562	      (SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf)
1563	
1564	/-- **Left unitor for `⊗_X`.** `𝒪_X ⊗_X M ≅ M`. (Blueprint
1565	`lem:tensorobj_unit_iso`, left half, `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.tensorObj_left_unitor`.)
1566	Sheafification of the presheaf-level left unitor `λ_ M.val`, composed with the
1567	sheafification counit identifying `sheafification M.val` with the (already-sheaf)
1568	`M`. The cheap `mapIso` pattern; uses no abstract pullback. -/
1569	noncomputable def tensorObj_left_unitor {X : Scheme.{u}} (M : X.Modules) :
1570	    tensorObj (SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf) M ≅ M :=
1571	  (PresheafOfModules.sheafification (R := X.ringCatSheaf) (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.val)).mapIso
1572	      ((PresheafOfModules.monoidalCategoryStruct (R := X.presheaf)).leftUnitor M.val) ≪≫
1573	    (asIso (PresheafOfModules.sheafificationAdjunction (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.val)).counit).app M
1574	
1575	/-- **Right unitor for `⊗_X`.** `M ⊗_X 𝒪_X ≅ M`. (Blueprint
1576	`lem:tensorobj_unit_iso`, right half, `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.tensorObj_right_unitor`.)
1577	Sheafification of the presheaf-level right unitor `ρ_ M.val`, composed with the
1578	sheafification counit. -/
1579	noncomputable def tensorObj_right_unitor {X : Scheme.{u}} (M : X.Modules) :
1580	    tensorObj M (SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf) ≅ M :=
1581	  (PresheafOfModules.sheafification (R := X.ringCatSheaf) (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.val)).mapIso
1582	      ((PresheafOfModules.monoidalCategoryStruct (R := X.presheaf)).rightUnitor M.val) ≪≫
1583	    (asIso (PresheafOfModules.sheafificationAdjunction (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.val)).counit).app M
1584	
1585	/-- **Braiding for `⊗_X`.** `M ⊗_X N ≅ N ⊗_X M`. (Blueprint
1586	`lem:tensorobj_comm_iso`, `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.tensorObj_braiding`.)
1587	The presheaf-of-modules monoidal category is symmetric; its braiding `β_ M.val
1588	N.val` sheafifies to the asserted isomorphism by the cheap `mapIso` pattern. -/
1589	noncomputable def tensorObj_braiding {X : Scheme.{u}} (M N : X.Modules) :
1590	    tensorObj M N ≅ tensorObj N M :=
1591	  (PresheafOfModules.sheafification (R := X.ringCatSheaf) (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.val)).mapIso
1592	    (BraidedCategory.braiding
1593	      (C := _root_.PresheafOfModules (X.presheaf ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)) M.val N.val)
1594	
1595	/-- **Associator for `⊗_X` on `⊗`-invertible objects.** (Blueprint
1596	`lem:tensorobj_assoc_iso`, `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.tensorObj_assoc_iso`.)
1597	For `M, N, P` `⊗`-invertible (hence locally free of rank one, hence sectionwise

1599	objectwise existence-of-iso datum the group law
1600	`tensorObjIsoclassCommMonoid` consumes (associativity as a `Nonempty (… ≅ …)`).

1468	/-! ## §1. The substrate tensor-product operation -/
1469	
1470	/-- **The substrate operation `⊗` on `Scheme.Modules X`.**
1471	
1472	For a scheme `X` and `M, N : X.Modules`, the tensor product
1473	`M ⊗_X N : X.Modules` is the sheafification of the presheaf-of-modules tensor
1474	product `PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj` of the underlying presheaves of
1475	`M` and `N` (affine-locally `(M ⊗_X N)(Spec A) = M(Spec A) ⊗_A N(Spec A)`).
1476	
1477	Per blueprint `def:scheme_modules_tensorobj`. The body lifts
1478	`PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj` through the sheafification functor on
1479	the small Zariski site of `X` (fully defined, no `sorry`). -/
1480	noncomputable def tensorObj {X : Scheme.{u}} (M N : X.Modules) : X.Modules :=
1481	  ((PresheafOfModules.sheafification (R := X.ringCatSheaf) (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.val)).obj
1482	      (PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj (R := X.presheaf) M.val N.val) :
1483	    SheafOfModules X.ringCatSheaf)
1484	
1485	/-- **Functoriality of `⊗_X`.**
1486	
1487	A pair of morphisms `f : M ⟶ M'` and `g : N ⟶ N'` in `X.Modules` determines a
1488	morphism `f ⊗ g : tensorObj M N ⟶ tensorObj M' N'`, compatible with identities
1489	and composition; the assignment `(M, N) ↦ tensorObj M N` thereby extends to a
1490	bifunctor `X.Modules × X.Modules ⥤ X.Modules` natural in both arguments.
1491	
1492	Per blueprint `lem:scheme_modules_tensorobj_functoriality`. The body inherits
1493	the morphism action from `PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj` under
1494	sheafification (fully defined, no `sorry`). -/
1495	noncomputable def tensorObj_functoriality {X : Scheme.{u}} {M M' N N' : X.Modules}
1496	    (f : M ⟶ M') (g : N ⟶ N') : tensorObj M N ⟶ tensorObj M' N' :=
1497	  (PresheafOfModules.sheafification (R := X.ringCatSheaf) (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.val)).map
1498	    (MonoidalCategory.tensorHom
1499	      (C := _root_.PresheafOfModules (X.presheaf ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)) f.val g.val)
1500	
1501	/-- **`⊗`-invertibility of an `𝒪_X`-module.** (Blueprint
1502	`def:scheme_modules_isinvertible`.) `M : X.Modules` is `⊗`-invertible when it
1503	admits a tensor inverse: an object `N` with `M ⊗_X N ≅ 𝒪_X`, where
1504	`𝒪_X = SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf` is the designated unit. This is the
1505	scheme-level analogue of Mathlib's `Module.Invertible`; the predicate carries its
1506	inverse witness existentially, so the dual needed by the relative Picard group
1507	law is definitional. -/
1508	def IsInvertible {X : Scheme.{u}} (M : X.Modules) : Prop :=
1509	  ∃ N : X.Modules, Nonempty (tensorObj M N ≅ SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf)

1511	/-! ## §2. (Off the critical path) the full monoidal-category structure
1512	
1513	iter-206 PIVOT: the full `MonoidalCategory (X.Modules)` instance is
1514	**deliberately not built**. Per blueprint `rem:scheme_modules_monoidal_off_path`,
1515	the relative Picard group law is a structure on *isomorphism classes* of line
1516	bundles — every group axiom is a `Nonempty (… ≅ …)` proposition, so no monoidal
1517	coherence (pentagon/triangle/hexagon) and no `MonoidalClosed` structure is ever
1518	consumed. The earlier `monoidalCategory := sorry` instance (and the two
1519	localization-transport supplements `isMonoidal_W_of_whiskerLeft`,
1520	`monoidalCategoryOfIsMonoidalW`) routed through the verified-absent
1521	`MonoidalClosed (PresheafOfModules R₀)` wall; they are removed here. The group
1522	law is built directly on the line-bundle subcategory from the four
1523	existence-of-iso lemmas below, mirroring Mathlib's `CommRing.Pic`. -/
1524	
1525	/-! ## §3. The lift through `LineBundle.OnProduct` (PUSH-BEYOND supporting lemmas)
1526	
1527	The following helper lemmas record the lift of the substrate to the
1528	locally-trivial subcategory used by the relative Picard consumer. They are not
1529	`\lean{...}`-pinned in the blueprint (their statements are descriptive); the
1530	typed signatures here scaffold the iter-203+ bodies. -/
1531	
1532	/-- **The substrate operation respects isomorphisms in both arguments.**
1533	
1534	A pair of isomorphisms `e : M ≅ M'` and `e' : N ≅ N'` in `X.Modules` induces an
1535	isomorphism `tensorObj M N ≅ tensorObj M' N'`, obtained by sheafifying the
1536	tensor product (in the presheaf-of-modules monoidal category) of the underlying
1537	presheaf isomorphisms `e.val`, `e'.val`. Its `hom` is
1538	`tensorObj_functoriality e.hom e'.hom`. This is the reusable functor-of-isos
1539	ingredient feeding `tensorObj_isLocallyTrivial`. -/
1540	noncomputable def tensorObjIsoOfIso {X : Scheme.{u}} {M M' N N' : X.Modules}
1541	    (e : M ≅ M') (e' : N ≅ N') : tensorObj M N ≅ tensorObj M' N' :=
1542	  (PresheafOfModules.sheafification (R := X.ringCatSheaf) (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.val)).mapIso
1543	    (MonoidalCategory.tensorIso
1544	      (C := _root_.PresheafOfModules (X.presheaf ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat))
1545	      ((SheafOfModules.forget X.ringCatSheaf).mapIso e)
1546	      ((SheafOfModules.forget X.ringCatSheaf).mapIso e'))

1549	sheaf.**

1552	designated unit object. Built from the presheaf-level left unitor
1553	`λ_ (𝟙_)` (the unit of the `PresheafOfModules` monoidal category is exactly
1554	`SheafOfModules.unit.val`) under sheafification, composed with the
1555	sheafification-adjunction counit isomorphism on the (already-sheaf) unit. -/

content: Answer questions?

The tools are now loaded — my earlier question was premature. Let me focus on the actual task: closing the `internalHomEval` naturality `sorry` at line 1456. Let me read the iter-222 handoff and the goal state.

1	# AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/TensorObjSubstrate.lean
2	
3	## Summary
4	- **Declarations added (2 axiom-clean):**
5	  - `PresheafOfModules.internalHomEvalApp_tmul` (≈L1421, `rfl`) — the contraction simp lemma.
6	  - `PresheafOfModules.restr_map_homMk` (≈L1434, `rfl`) — `(restr U N).map (Over.homMk f.unop).op = N.map f`.
7	  Both VERIFIED `#print axioms = {propext, Classical.choice, Quot.sound}`.
8	- **PRIMARY target `internalHomEval` (`lem:internal_hom_eval`): NOT landed** — the naturality
9	  reduction is **fully worked out and verified in pieces**, but the final assembly hits a `whnf`
10	  **heartbeat BOMB** (times out even at `maxHeartbeats 3200000`). Held as a typed `sorry`
11	  naturality field to keep the build **GREEN**; the complete worked proof + the `dual_map_app_terminal`
12	  lemma + the precise bomb diagnosis are preserved verbatim in a `/- … -/` block above the decl.
13	- **File status:** compiles **GREEN** (verified `errors=0`).
14	- **sorry count:** 3 → 4 (added `internalHomEval` naturality sorry; pre-existing 3:
15	  `isLocallyInjective_whiskerLeft_of_W`, `tensorObj_assoc_iso`, `exists_tensorObj_inverse`).
16	
17	## What is fully VERIFIED (each compiles axiom-clean in isolation, < 200000 heartbeats)
18	Confirmed via `lean_multi_attempt` and standalone compiles:
19	1. `internalHomEvalApp_tmul := rfl` — axiom-clean (landed).
20	2. `restr_map_homMk := rfl` (N abstract) — axiom-clean (landed). **Cheap only for abstract N.**
21	3. `dual_map_app_terminal` (`((dual M).map f φ).app term_Y = φ.app (op (Over.mk f.unop))`,
22	   proof = `hom_app_heq` + `congrArg Over.mk (Category.id_comp f.unop)`) — axiom-clean in isolation.
23	4. The reduction: after `intro X Y f`, `refine ModuleCat.MonoidalCategory.tensor_ext (fun s φ => ?_)`,
24	   then `change` to the contraction form (defeq-valid, confirmed), `erw [Monoidal.tensorObj_map_tmul]`,
25	   `rw [internalHomEvalApp_tmul, internalHomEvalApp_tmul]` reduces the naturality square to
26	   **G**: `evalLin M Y ((dual M).map f φ) (M.map f s) = ((𝟙_).map f).hom (evalLin M X φ s)`.
27	5. `key := PresheafOfModules.naturality_apply (φ : restr X.unop M ⟶ restr X.unop (𝟙_)) (Over.homMk f.unop).op s`
28	   elaborates fine; `rw [restr_map_homMk M f] at key` fires.
29	
30	## THE BLOCKER — `whnf` heartbeat bomb at the unit instantiation
31	The step `rw [restr_map_homMk (𝟙_ (PresheafOfModules …)) f] at key` (instantiating the `rfl`-bridge
32	at the **concrete unit object** `𝟙_`) and/or the final `exact key.symm` trigger a `whnf` explosion
33	of the deeply nested `ofPresheaf ∘ pushforward₀ ∘ Over.map ∘ restrictScalars ∘ tensorObj ∘ unit`
34	machinery — **> 3.2M heartbeats** (`(deterministic) timeout at whnf`). Diagnostic detail:
35	- `restr_map_homMk M f` with **M abstract** is cheap (verified). The explosion is specific to the
36	  **unit `𝟙_`** (its `whnf` normal form is enormous), so `restr_map_homMk (𝟙_) f` and any tactic
37	  that defeq-checks against the unit (`exact key.symm`, `change`/`from rfl` touching the unit) blow up.
38	- `set_option maxHeartbeats {1.6M, 3.2M}` all time out → not a budget issue, the cost is ~exponential.
39	
40	## NEXT ITER — tame the bomb (whnf-free assembly)
41	Concrete routes (any one should close it; all keep every step syntactic, never whnf the unit):
42	1. **Generalize the unit.** Before `naturality_apply`/`rw …at key`, `set U := 𝟙_ (PresheafOfModules …)`
43	   (or `generalize`), do the `restr_map_homMk`/`naturality_apply`/`exact` with `U` ABSTRACT (cheap),
44	   then `subst`/specialize `U := 𝟙_` only at the very end where no further whnf is forced.
45	2. **Use Mathlib's pushforward map lemmas** instead of the `rfl`-bridge so matching is syntactic:
46	   `PresheafOfModules.pushforward_obj_map_apply'` / `pushforward_map_app_apply'`
47	   (`Mathlib.Algebra.Category.ModuleCat.Presheaf.Pushforward`) —
48	   `(((pushforward φ).obj M).map f) m = (M.map (F.map f.unop).op) m`. Rewrite `key` with these
49	   (note `pushforward₀ F R = pushforward (𝟙 (F.op ⋙ R))`).
50	3. **Close G elementwise without `exact key.symm`:** after syntactic `rw [dual_map_app_terminal,
51	   restr_map_homMk]` (avoiding the unit-instantiation form), finish with `LinearMap.ext` / `congr 1`
52	   / `exact (key …)` where every remaining goal is between small (`evalLin`/`φ.app`) terms.
53	
54	The reduction is done; only this elaboration-cost issue remains. Expected ≤ 1 focused iter.
55	
56	## Why I stopped
57	Two compounding factors: (a) a persistent tooling-output lag (LSP/Bash results returned only in
58	large delayed batches, so each edit→compile→read cycle cost minutes — I worked around it with
59	background `lake env lean` runs writing bare error-counts); (b) a genuine `whnf` heartbeat bomb in
60	the final assembly that exceeds 3.2M heartbeats and is not fixable by bumping the budget. With the
61	reduction fully verified in pieces and the bomb precisely localized to the unit-`𝟙_` instantiation,
62	the responsible outcome is a GREEN build (typed `sorry`) + the complete worked proof preserved
63	in-source + this sharp handoff. This is a materially stronger position than iter-221 ("blocked on
64	Over.map coherence"): the coherence is solved (`restr_map_homMk`, `dual_map_app_terminal`,
65	axiom-clean), and only an elaboration-cost obstacle with three concrete fixes remains.
66	
67	**Real progress:** 2 axiom-clean declarations (`internalHomEvalApp_tmul`, `restr_map_homMk`) +
68	a verified reduction of `internalHomEval` naturality to a single whnf-cost obstacle.
69	
70	## Blueprint markers
71	- `lem:internal_hom_eval` (`internalHomEval`): NOT ready for `\leanok` (naturality `sorry`).
72	- `internalHomEvalApp_tmul`, `restr_map_homMk`: supporting lemmas (no dedicated pins).
73

973	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — the internal hom of presheaves of modules
974	(slice formula): the `R(T)`-module structure on `Hom(M, N)`
975	
976	This section builds the FIRST primitive of the sheaf internal-hom / dual block
977	(blueprint `sec:tensorobj_dual_infra`, the `⊗`-inverse's missing ingredient): the
978	`R(T)`-module structure on the morphism abelian group `M ⟶ N` of presheaves of
979	modules over a base category `C` with a **terminal object** `T`, where the scalar
980	ring is the global ring `R(T)`.
981	
982	This is exactly the module attached to each value of the slice internal hom
983	`ℋom(M, N)(U) := ModuleCat.of (R(U)) (M|_U ⟶ N|_U)` of
984	blueprint `def:presheaf_internal_hom`: over the restricted site (terminal `U`),
985	the section module over `U` is `Hom(M|_U, N|_U)` with its `R(U)`-action. The slice
986	formula is forced by contravariance of the naive pointwise rule
987	`U ↦ Hom_{R(U)}(M(U), N(U))`; the module of morphisms of *restricted* objects is
988	the covariant remedy, and its `R(U)`-module structure is the content here.
989	
990	The action is `f • φ := φ ≫ globalSMul f`, where `globalSMul f : N ⟶ N` is the
991	"multiply by the global scalar `f ∈ R(T)`" endomorphism: at an object `Y`, with
992	`R(T) → R(Y)` the canonical map `termRingMap` induced by the unique `Y → T`, it is
993	scalar multiplication by the image of `f`. Mathlib has the fixed-ring internal hom
994	`ihom M N = (M ⟶ N)` (`Mathlib/Algebra/Category/ModuleCat/Monoidal/Closed.lean`) but
995	nothing for the varying structure sheaf at the `PresheafOfModules` level; this is the
996	project-local supplement. -/
997	
998	namespace InternalHom
999	
1000	open CategoryTheory Limits
1001	
1002	universe vC uC
1003	
1004	variable {C : Type uC} [Category.{vC} C] {R : Cᵒᵖ ⥤ CommRingCat.{u}}
1005	  {T : C} (hT : IsTerminal T)
1006	
1007	/-- **The canonical ring map `R(T) → R(Y)` from a terminal object `T`.** For each
1008	object `Y`, the unique morphism `Y.unop → T` (terminality) induces, after `op` and
1009	applying `R`, the ring map `R(T) → R(Y)` along which a global scalar `f ∈ R(T)` acts
1010	on `R(Y)`-modules. Project-local: the `R(T)`-module structure on `Hom(M, N)` (the slice
1011	internal-hom value) is defined through this map. -/
1012	noncomputable def termRingMap (Y : Cᵒᵖ) : R.obj (Opposite.op T) ⟶ R.obj Y :=
1013	  R.map (hT.from Y.unop).op
1014	
1015	/-- **Naturality of `termRingMap`.** The restriction map `R(g) : R(X) → R(Y)` of `R`
1016	along `g : X ⟶ Y` carries `termRingMap X f` to `termRingMap Y f`; equivalently the
1017	images of a global scalar `f ∈ R(T)` are compatible with restriction. This is the
1018	sole ingredient making `globalSMul f` a genuine morphism of presheaves of modules. -/
1019	lemma termRingMap_naturality {X Y : Cᵒᵖ} (g : X ⟶ Y) (f) :
1020	    (ConcreteCategory.hom ((R ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat).map g))
1021	        ((ConcreteCategory.hom (termRingMap hT X)) f)
1022	      = (ConcreteCategory.hom (termRingMap hT Y)) f := by
1023	  have h : (hT.from X.unop).op ≫ g = (hT.from Y.unop).op := by
1024	    apply Quiver.Hom.unop_inj; apply hT.hom_ext
1025	  change (ConcreteCategory.hom (R.map g)) ((ConcreteCategory.hom (R.map (hT.from X.unop).op)) f) = _
1026	  rw [show termRingMap hT Y = R.map (hT.from Y.unop).op from rfl, ← h, Functor.map_comp]; rfl
1027	
1028	/-- **Multiplication by a global scalar `f ∈ R(T)` as an endomorphism of `N`.** At an
1029	object `Y`, it is scalar multiplication by `termRingMap Y f ∈ R(Y)` on `N(Y)`; the
1030	naturality square commutes by `termRingMap_naturality` and the semilinearity of the
1031	restriction maps of `N` (`PresheafOfModules.map_smul`). This is the central object: it
1032	gives the `R(T)`-action on `Hom(M, N)` by post-composition (`homModule`). -/
1033	noncomputable def globalSMul (N : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat))
1034	    (f : (R.obj (Opposite.op T) : Type u)) : N ⟶ N :=
1035	  PresheafOfModules.Hom.mk
1036	    (fun Y => ModuleCat.ofHom
1037	      (LinearMap.lsmul (R.obj Y : Type u) (N.obj Y) ((ConcreteCategory.hom (termRingMap hT Y)) f)))

946	      (fun _ _ φ => g.naturality φ)).hom :=

1051	lemma globalSMul_hom_apply (f : (R.obj (Opposite.op T) : Type u)) (Y : Cᵒᵖ) (m : N.obj Y) :
1052	    ((globalSMul hT N f).app Y).hom m = ((ConcreteCategory.hom (termRingMap hT Y)) f) • m := rfl
1053	
1054	/-- `globalSMul 1 = 𝟙`. -/
1055	lemma globalSMul_one : globalSMul hT N 1 = 𝟙 N := by
1056	  ext Y m; rw [globalSMul_hom_apply, map_one, one_smul]; rfl
1057	
1058	/-- `globalSMul 0 = 0`. -/
1059	lemma globalSMul_zero : globalSMul hT N 0 = 0 := by
1060	  ext Y m; rw [globalSMul_hom_apply, map_zero, zero_smul]; rfl
1061	
1062	/-- `globalSMul` is additive in the scalar. -/
1063	lemma globalSMul_add (f g : (R.obj (Opposite.op T) : Type u)) :
1064	    globalSMul hT N (f + g) = globalSMul hT N f + globalSMul hT N g := by
1065	  ext Y m; rw [add_app, globalSMul_hom_apply, _root_.map_add, add_smul]; rfl
1066	
1067	/-- `globalSMul` turns ring multiplication into composition (the scalar endomorphisms
1068	commute, so the order is immaterial). -/
1069	lemma globalSMul_mul (f g : (R.obj (Opposite.op T) : Type u)) :
1070	    globalSMul hT N (f * g) = globalSMul hT N g ≫ globalSMul hT N f := by
1071	  ext Y m; rw [comp_app, globalSMul_hom_apply, map_mul, mul_smul, ModuleCat.hom_comp,
1072	    LinearMap.comp_apply, globalSMul_hom_apply, globalSMul_hom_apply]
1073	
1074	/-- **The `R(T)`-module structure on `Hom(M, N)` of presheaves of modules over a base
1075	category `C` with a terminal object `T`.** The action `f • φ := φ ≫ globalSMul f` scales
1076	a morphism by post-composing with multiplication by the global scalar `f ∈ R(T)`; the
1077	module axioms reduce to the `globalSMul_{one,zero,add,mul}` ring-homomorphism facts and
1078	the bilinearity of composition (`Preadditive`). This is the module carried by each value
1079	of the slice internal hom `ℋom(M, N)(U)` of blueprint `def:presheaf_internal_hom`
1080	(take `C =` the restricted site over `U`, with terminal `U`, so `R(T) = R(U)`).
1081	Project-local: Mathlib has the fixed-ring internal hom but no `PresheafOfModules`-level
1082	internal hom for the varying structure sheaf. -/
1083	@[implicit_reducible]
1084	noncomputable def homModule (M N : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)) :
1085	    Module (R.obj (Opposite.op T) : Type u) (M ⟶ N) where
1086	  smul f φ := φ ≫ globalSMul hT N f
1087	  one_smul φ := by change φ ≫ globalSMul hT N 1 = φ; rw [globalSMul_one, Category.comp_id]
1088	  mul_smul f g φ := by
1089	    change φ ≫ globalSMul hT N (f * g) = (φ ≫ globalSMul hT N g) ≫ globalSMul hT N f
1090	    rw [globalSMul_mul, Category.assoc]
1091	  smul_zero f := by change (0 : M ⟶ N) ≫ globalSMul hT N f = 0; rw [Limits.zero_comp]
1092	  zero_smul φ := by change φ ≫ globalSMul hT N 0 = 0; rw [globalSMul_zero, Limits.comp_zero]
1093	  smul_add f φ ψ := by
1094	    change (φ + ψ) ≫ globalSMul hT N f = φ ≫ globalSMul hT N f + ψ ≫ globalSMul hT N f
1095	    rw [Preadditive.add_comp]
1096	  add_smul f g φ := by
1097	    change φ ≫ globalSMul hT N (f + g) = φ ≫ globalSMul hT N f + φ ≫ globalSMul hT N g
1098	    rw [globalSMul_add, Preadditive.comp_add]

1100	/-! ### The slice value at an object `U` via the over-category `Over U`
1101	
1102	The slice internal hom `ℋom(M, N)(U) := ModuleCat.of (R(U)) (M|_U ⟶ N|_U)` of
1103	blueprint `def:presheaf_internal_hom` is realized object-by-object here. The
1104	restriction `M|_U` is `PresheafOfModules.pushforward₀` along the over-category
1105	projection `Over.forget U : Over U ⥤ C`; the over-category has terminal object
1106	`Over.mk (𝟙 U)` (`Over.mkIdTerminal`), at which the restricted ring is `R(U)`
1107	(by `rfl`), so the `R(U)`-module structure on `M|_U ⟶ N|_U` is exactly `homModule`
1108	applied to that terminal. -/
1109	
1110	/-- **Restriction of a presheaf of modules to the over-category `Over U`.** This is
1111	the `M|_U` of the slice internal-hom formula: `PresheafOfModules.pushforward₀` along
1112	the over-category projection `Over.forget U`. The new base presheaf of rings is
1113	`(Over.forget U).op ⋙ R`, whose value at the terminal `Over.mk (𝟙 U)` is `R(U)`. -/
1114	noncomputable def restr (U : C)
1115	    (M : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)) :
1116	    PresheafOfModules.{u} (((Over.forget U).op ⋙ R) ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat) :=
1117	  (PresheafOfModules.pushforward₀ (Over.forget U) (R ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)).obj M
1118	
1119	/-- **The slice internal-hom value at `U`: `Hom(M|_U, N|_U)` as an `R(U)`-module.**
1120	This is the `R(U)`-module underlying `ℋom(M, N)(U)` of blueprint
1121	`def:presheaf_internal_hom`: the morphism group of the over-category restrictions
1122	`M|_U ⟶ N|_U`, equipped with the `homModule` action for the terminal object
1123	`Over.mk (𝟙 U)` of `Over U`. The full presheaf `internalHom` (the assembly of these
1124	values over the restriction maps `V ⟶ U`) was assembled iter-220 (`internalHom`). -/
1125	@[implicit_reducible]
1126	noncomputable def internalHomObjModule (U : C)

content:
noncomputable def internalHomEval$
    (M : PresheafOfModules.{u} (RM-bM-^BM-^@ M-bM-^KM-^Y forgetM-bM-^BM-^B CommRingCat RingCat)) :$
    PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj M (dual M) M-bM-^_M-6$
      M-pM-^]M-^_M-^Y_ (PresheafOfModules.{u} (RM-bM-^BM-^@ M-bM-^KM-^Y forgetM-bM-^BM-^B CommRingCat RingCat)) :=$
  PresheafOfModules.Hom.mk (fun X => internalHomEvalApp M X) (by$
    intro X Y f$
    refine ModuleCat.MonoidalCategory.tensor_ext (fun s M-OM-^F => ?_)$
    simp only [ModuleCat.comp_apply]$
    erw [PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj_map_tmul]$
    rw [internalHomEvalApp_tmul, internalHomEvalApp_tmul]$
    -- Goal G: evalLin M Y (dual.map f M-OM-^F) (M.map f s) = ((M-pM-^]M-^_M-^Y_).map f).hom (evalLin M X M-OM-^F s)$
    erw [PresheafOfModules.unit_map_apply]$
    -- Goal G': evalLin M Y (dual.map f M-OM-^F) (M.map f s) = (R.map f) (evalLin M X M-OM-^F s)$
    -- (M-bM-^XM-^E): the restricted-unit transition map is the ring map (whnf-free, via pushforward lemmas).$
    have star : M-bM-^HM-^@ r : (RM-bM-^BM-^@.obj X : Type u),$
        ((PresheafOfModules.restr X.unop$
            (M-pM-^]M-^_M-^Y_ (PresheafOfModules.{u} (RM-bM-^BM-^@ M-bM-^KM-^Y forgetM-bM-^BM-^B CommRingCat RingCat)))).map$
              (Over.homMk f.unop : Over.mk f.unop M-bM-^_M-6 Over.mk (M-pM-^]M-^_M-^Y X.unop)).op).hom r$
          = (ConcreteCategory.hom ((RM-bM-^BM-^@ M-bM-^KM-^Y forgetM-bM-^BM-^B CommRingCat RingCat).map f)) r := by$
      intro r$
      unfold PresheafOfModules.restr$
      rw [show PresheafOfModules.pushforwardM-bM-^BM-^@ (Over.forget X.unop)$
            = PresheafOfModules.pushforward$
                (M-pM-^]M-^_M-^Y ((Over.forget X.unop).op M-bM-^KM-^Y RM-bM-^BM-^@ M-bM-^KM-^Y forgetM-bM-^BM-^B CommRingCat RingCat)) from rfl]$
      erw [PresheafOfModules.pushforward_obj_map_apply, PresheafOfModules.unit_map_apply]$
    sorry)$
$
end Dual$
$
end PresheafOfModules$
$
namespace AlgebraicGeometry$
$
namespace Scheme$

simp only [PresheafOfModules.pushforward_obj_map_apply, Over.forget_map, Over.homMk_left,
        PresheafOfModules.unit_map_apply]
    -- naturality of the dual section `φ` over the restricted site, for the morphism
    -- `(Over.homMk f.unop).op : op (Over.mk (𝟙 X.unop)) ⟶ op (Over.mk f.unop)`.
    have key := PresheafOfModules.naturality_apply
      (φ : PresheafOfModules.restr X.unop M ⟶ PresheafOfModules.restr X.unop
        (𝟙_ (PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat))))
      (Over.homMk f.unop : Over.mk f.unop ⟶ Over.mk (𝟙 X.unop)).op s
    rw [restr_map_homMk M f] at key
    sorry)

input.old_string:
rw [restr_map_homMk M f] at key
    sorry)
input.new_string:
rw [restr_map_homMk M f] at key
    -- `(dual.map f φ)` evaluated at the terminal section of `Over Y.unop` is `φ` evaluated at
    -- `op (Over.mk f.unop)`: the further-restriction reindexes the over-category along `Over.map`,
    -- and `Over.mk (𝟙 Y.unop ≫ f.unop) = Over.mk f.unop` by `id_comp` (whnf-free: M abstract).
    have hdt : (((dual M).map f φ).app (Opposite.op (Over.mk (𝟙 Y.unop)))).hom
             = (φ.app (Opposite.op (Over.mk f.unop))).hom := by
      apply congrArg ModuleCat.Hom.hom
      exact eq_of_heq (hom_app_heq φ
        (congrArg Opposite.op (congrArg Over.mk (Category.id_comp f.unop))))
    have hdt2 : evalLin M Y ((dual M).map f φ) (M.map f s)
             = (φ.app (Opposite.op (Over.mk f.unop))).hom (M.map f s) := by
      show (((dual M).map f φ).app (Opposite.op (Over.mk (𝟙 Y.unop)))).hom (M.map f s) = _
      rw [hdt]
    rw [hdt2]
    exact key.trans (star (evalLin M X φ s)))

# AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/TensorObjSubstrate.lean

## Summary
- **sorry count: 4 → 3.** Closed the PRIMARY target: the `naturality` field of
  `PresheafOfModules.internalHomEval` (was the typed `sorry` at L1456, iter-222 whnf bomb).
- **`internalHomEval` is axiom-clean:** `lean_verify` →
  `{propext, Classical.choice, Quot.sound}`, `sorry_or_admit: false`, `status: clean`.
- **Build GREEN** (full-file `lean_diagnostic_messages severity=error` → `[]`).
- **Sub-step 3 of the dual block RETIRED.**
- Remaining sorries (unchanged, all pre-existing / out of scope this iter): L602
  `isLocallyInjective_whiskerLeft_of_W`, L1879 `exists_tensorObj_inverse`, L1929
  `addCommGroup_via_tensorObj`.

## internalHomEval naturality (was L1456) — RESOLVED
### Approach (PRIMARY ROUTE #2 + #3, whnf-free)
The iter-222 reduction was correct; the only obstacle was the `whnf` heartbeat bomb at the
concrete unit `𝟙_` when matching `((restr X.unop (𝟙_)).map g)` against `(𝟙_).map f` via the
`rfl`-bridge `restr_map_homMk (𝟙_) f`. I sidestepped it entirely by computing **each** side to a
common `R.map f` form with syntactic Mathlib lemmas, never defeq-checking the two unit forms
against each other.

Proof skeleton (all verified in-source):
1. `simp only [ModuleCat.comp_apply]; erw [PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj_map_tmul];
   rw [internalHomEvalApp_tmul, internalHomEvalApp_tmul]` reduces the square to
   **G**: `evalLin M Y (dual.map f φ) (M.map f s) = ((𝟙_).map f).hom (evalLin M X φ s)`.
2. `erw [PresheafOfModules.unit_map_apply]` rewrites the RHS unit map to the ring map (cheap —
   does NOT bomb), giving **G'**: `… = (R.map f) (evalLin M X φ s)`.
3. **(★)** `have star : ((restr X.unop (𝟙_)).map (Over.homMk f.unop).op).hom r = (R.map f) r`,
   proved by `unfold restr; rw [show pushforward₀ … = pushforward (𝟙 …) from rfl];
   simp only [pushforward_obj_map_apply, Over.forget_map, Over.homMk_left, unit_map_apply]`.
   This is the whnf-free replacement for `restr_map_homMk (𝟙_) f` — the pushforward map-action
   lemma `PresheafOfModules.pushforward_obj_map_apply` rewrites the restricted-unit transition
   map syntactically, then `unit_map_apply` lands it on `R.map f`.
4. `have key := naturality_apply φ (Over.homMk f.unop).op s; rw [restr_map_homMk M f] at key`
   (the **M-abstract** form of `restr_map_homMk`, which is cheap).
5. **`dual_map_app_terminal`** as `have hdt`: `((dual M).map f φ).app (op (Over.mk (𝟙 Y.unop))).hom
   = (φ.app (op (Over.mk f.unop))).hom`, via `congrArg ModuleCat.Hom.hom (eq_of_heq (hom_app_heq φ
   (congrArg op (congrArg Over.mk (Category.id_comp f.unop)))))`. (The further-restriction reindexes
   the over-category along `Over.map f.unop`, and `Over.mk (𝟙 Y.unop ≫ f.unop) = Over.mk f.unop`.)
6. Assembly: `exact hdt2.trans (key.trans (star (evalLin M X φ s)))` where `hdt2` lifts `hdt`
   through `evalLin`'s defeq. **`Eq.trans` + `exact` close up to defeq**, so the expensive
   `restr_map_homMk (𝟙_)` defeq is never triggered — this is the crux of the bomb avoidance.

### Key insight / reusable tricks
- `erw [PresheafOfModules.unit_map_apply]` rewrites `(𝟙_).map f` → `R.map f` WITHOUT bombing
  (unlike `restr_map_homMk (𝟙_) f`).
- The restricted-unit transition map `(restr U (𝟙_)).map g` is computed whnf-free via
  `pushforward_obj_map_apply` (after `unfold restr` + `rw [show pushforward₀ = pushforward (𝟙 _)
  from rfl]`) — NOT via the `rfl`-bridge.
- Close the final square by `Eq.trans`/`exact` (defeq-checked), never `rw`, so no syntactic
  `ConcreteCategory.hom` vs `ModuleCat.Hom.hom` friction and no unit defeq blow-up.

### Dead ends
- `rw [restr_map_homMk (𝟙_) f]` and any `rfl`/`change`/`exact` defeq-checking
  `(restr U (𝟙_)).map g` against `(𝟙_).map f`: confirmed bomb (iter-222), avoided.
- `erw [pushforward_obj_map_apply]` does NOT fire (keyed match on `pushforward₀` head); must
  `rw [show pushforward₀ … = pushforward (𝟙 …) from rfl]` first, then `simp only`.
- `rw [hdt2]` fails (`ConcreteCategory.hom` vs `.hom` syntactic mismatch in the goal); use
  `exact hdt2.trans …` instead.

## Blueprint markers
- `lem:internal_hom_eval` (`PresheafOfModules.internalHomEval`): now PROOF-complete and axiom-clean
  → ready for `\leanok` on its proof block (applied deterministically by `sync_leanok`).

## Why I stopped
**Real progress: closed 1 sorry** (`internalHomEval` naturality), file 4 → 3, axiom-clean,
build GREEN. Sub-step 3 of the dual block retired. The three remaining sorries
(`isLocallyInjective_whiskerLeft_of_W`, `exists_tensorObj_inverse`, `addCommGroup_via_tensorObj`)
are explicitly out of scope / FORBIDDEN this iter per the objectives (sub-step 5 + RPF consumer +
route-(e) whisker residual). No adjacent in-scope sorry remained to attempt.

1. `simp only [ModuleCat.comp_apply]; erw [Monoidal.tensorObj_map_tmul];
   rw [internalHomEvalApp_tmul, internalHomEvalApp_tmul]` → reduces the square to
   **G**: `evalLin M Y (dual.map f φ) (M.map f s) = ((𝟙_).map f).hom (evalLin M X φ s)`.
2. `erw [PresheafOfModules.unit_map_apply]` rewrites the RHS unit map to the ring map (does NOT
   bomb), giving **G'**: `… = (R.map f) (evalLin M X φ s)`.
3. **(★)** `have star : ∀ r, ((restr X.unop (𝟙_)).map (Over.homMk f.unop).op).hom r = (R.map f) r`,
   proved by `intro r; unfold restr; rw [show pushforward₀ … = pushforward (𝟙 …) from rfl];
   simp only [pushforward_obj_map_apply, Over.forget_map, Over.homMk_left, unit_map_apply]`.
   This is the whnf-free replacement for `restr_map_homMk (𝟙_) f`: the pushforward map-action lemma
   `PresheafOfModules.pushforward_obj_map_apply` rewrites the restricted-unit transition map
   syntactically, then `unit_map_apply` lands it on `R.map f` — the unit's `whnf` normal form is
   never forced. (Note: `erw [pushforward_obj_map_apply]` does NOT fire — needs the explicit
   `rw [show pushforward₀ = pushforward (𝟙 _) from rfl]` first, then `simp only`.)
4. `have key := naturality_apply φ (Over.homMk f.unop).op s; rw [restr_map_homMk M f] at key`
   (the **M-abstract** form of `restr_map_homMk`, which is cheap).
5. **`dual_map_app_terminal`** as `have hdt`:
   `(((dual M).map f φ).app (op (Over.mk (𝟙 Y.unop)))).hom = (φ.app (op (Over.mk f.unop))).hom`,
   via `congrArg ModuleCat.Hom.hom (eq_of_heq (hom_app_heq φ (congrArg op (congrArg Over.mk
   (Category.id_comp f.unop)))))`. (The further-restriction reindexes the over-category by
   `Over.map f.unop`; `Over.mk (𝟙 Y.unop ≫ f.unop) = Over.mk f.unop` by `id_comp`.) Lifted through
   `evalLin`'s defeq as `hdt2` with `show … = _; rw [hdt]`.
6. Assembly: `exact hdt2.trans (key.trans (star (evalLin M X φ s)))`. `Eq.trans` + `exact` close
   up to defeq, so the expensive `restr_map_homMk (𝟙_)` defeq is never triggered.

### Reusable tricks (for the rest of the dual block / sheaf internal-hom build)
- `erw [PresheafOfModules.unit_map_apply]` rewrites `(𝟙_).map f` → `R.map f` WITHOUT bombing
  (unlike `restr_map_homMk (𝟙_) f`).
- The restricted-unit transition map `(restr U (𝟙_)).map g` is computed whnf-free via
  `unfold restr` + `rw [show pushforward₀ = pushforward (𝟙 _) from rfl]` +
  `simp only [pushforward_obj_map_apply, Over.forget_map, Over.homMk_left, unit_map_apply]`.
- Close the final naturality square by `Eq.trans`/`exact` (defeq), never `rw` — avoids both the
  unit defeq blow-up and the `ConcreteCategory.hom` vs `ModuleCat.Hom.hom` syntactic friction.

### Dead ends (avoid re-trying)
- `rw [restr_map_homMk (𝟙_) f]` / any `rfl`/`change`/`exact` defeq-checking `(restr U (𝟙_)).map g`
  against `(𝟙_).map f`: the iter-222 bomb (>3.2M heartbeats), confirmed, avoided.
- `erw [pushforward_obj_map_apply]` directly on a `pushforward₀` term: no keyed match.
- `rw [hdt2]`: fails (`ConcreteCategory.hom` vs `.hom` mismatch in goal); use `exact hdt2.trans …`.

## Blueprint markers
- `lem:internal_hom_eval` (`PresheafOfModules.internalHomEval`): now PROOF-complete and axiom-clean
  → ready for `\leanok` on its proof block (applied deterministically by `sync_leanok`).
- Ride-along (comment-only): updated the file-header `## Status (current)` block and the
  `internalHomEval` docstring to reflect the completed eval morphism (no proof-body changes).

## Why I stopped
**Real progress: closed 1 sorry** (`internalHomEval` naturality), file 4 → 3, axiom-clean, build
GREEN, sub-step 3 retired. The three remaining sorries (`isLocallyInjective_whiskerLeft_of_W`,
`exists_tensorObj_inverse`, `addCommGroup_via_tensorObj`) are explicitly out of scope / FORBIDDEN
this iter per the objectives (route-(e) whisker residual + sub-step 5 + RPF consumer). No
adjacent in-scope sorry remained to attempt.

input.new_string:
(𝟙 ((Over.forget X.unop).op ⋙ R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)) from rfl,
        PresheafOfModules.pushforward_obj_map_apply, PresheafOfModules.unit_map_apply]
    -- naturality of the dual section `φ` over the restricted site, for the morphism
    -- `(Over.homMk f.unop).op : op (Over.mk (𝟙 X.unop)) ⟶ op (Over.mk f.unop)`; rewrite the
    -- `M`-side further-restriction `(restr X.unop M).map _` to `M.map f` (cheap: `M` abstract).
    have key := PresheafOfModules.naturality_apply
      (φ : PresheafOfModules.restr X.unop M ⟶ PresheafOfModules.restr X.unop
        (𝟙_ (PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat))))
      (Over.homMk f.unop : Over.mk f.unop ⟶ Over.mk (𝟙 X.unop)).op s
    rw [restr_map_homMk M f] at key
    -- `(dual.map f φ)` at the terminal section of `Over Y.unop` is `φ` at `op (Over.mk f.unop)`:
    -- the further-restriction reindexes the over-category along `Over.map f.unop`, and
    -- `Over.mk (𝟙 Y.unop ≫ f.unop) = Over.mk f.unop` (whnf-free: `hom_app_heq` + `id_comp`).
    have hdt : (((dual M).map f φ).app (Opposite.op (Over.mk (𝟙 Y.unop)))).hom
             = (φ.app (Opposite.op (Over.mk f.unop))).hom := by
      apply congrArg ModuleCat.Hom.hom
      exact eq_of_heq (hom_app_heq φ
        (congrArg Opposite.op (congrArg Over.mk (Category.id_comp f.unop))))
    have hdt2 : evalLin M Y ((dual M).map f φ) ((ConcreteCategory.hom (M.map f)) s)
             = (φ.app (Opposite.op (Over.mk f.unop))).hom ((ConcreteCategory.hom (M.map f)) s) := by
      show (((dual M).map f φ).app (Opposite.op (Over.mk (𝟙 Y.unop)))).hom _ = _
      rw [hdt]
    exact hdt2.trans (key.trans (star (evalLin M X φ s))))

Proof skeleton (verified in-source, lines ≈1453–1495):
1. `simp only [ModuleCat.comp_apply]; erw [Monoidal.tensorObj_map_tmul];
   rw [internalHomEvalApp_tmul, internalHomEvalApp_tmul]` → reduces the square to
   **G**: `evalLin M Y (dual.map f φ) (M.map f s) = ((𝟙_).map f).hom (evalLin M X φ s)`.
2. `erw [PresheafOfModules.unit_map_apply]` rewrites the RHS unit map to the ring map (does NOT
   bomb), giving **G'**: `… = (R.map f) (evalLin M X φ s)`.
3. **(★)** `have star : ∀ r, ((restr X.unop (𝟙_)).map (Over.homMk f.unop).op).hom r = (R.map f) r`,
   proved by `intro r; unfold restr; rw [show pushforward₀ … = pushforward (𝟙 …) from rfl];
   simp only [pushforward_obj_map_apply, Over.forget_map, Over.homMk_left, unit_map_apply]`.
   This is the whnf-free replacement for `restr_map_homMk (𝟙_) f`: the pushforward map-action lemma
   `PresheafOfModules.pushforward_obj_map_apply` rewrites the restricted-unit transition map
   syntactically, then `unit_map_apply` lands it on `R.map f` — the unit's `whnf` normal form is
   never forced. (Note: `erw [pushforward_obj_map_apply]` does NOT fire — needs the explicit
   `rw [show pushforward₀ = pushforward (𝟙 _) from rfl]` first, then `simp only`.)
4. `have key := naturality_apply φ (Over.homMk f.unop).op s; rw [restr_map_homMk M f] at key`
   (the **M-abstract** form of `restr_map_homMk`, which is cheap).
5. **`dual_map_app_terminal`** as `have hdt`:
   `(((dual M).map f φ).app (op (Over.mk (𝟙 Y.unop)))).hom = (φ.app (op (Over.mk f.unop))).hom`,
   via `congrArg ModuleCat.Hom.hom (eq_of_heq (hom_app_heq φ (congrArg op (congrArg Over.mk
   (Category.id_comp f.unop)))))`. (The further-restriction reindexes the over-category by
   `Over.map f.unop`; `Over.mk (𝟙 Y.unop ≫ f.unop) = Over.mk f.unop` by `id_comp`.) Lifted through
   `evalLin`'s defeq as `hdt2` with `show … = _; rw [hdt]`.
6. Assembly: `exact hdt2.trans (key.trans (star (evalLin M X φ s)))`. `Eq.trans` + `exact` close
   up to defeq, so the expensive `restr_map_homMk (𝟙_)` defeq is never triggered.

3. **(★)** `have star : ∀ r, ((restr X.unop (𝟙_)).map (Over.homMk f.unop).op).hom r = (R.map f) r`,

   pushforward (𝟙 …) from rfl, pushforward_obj_map_apply, unit_map_apply]`. The whnf-free
   replacement for `restr_map_homMk (𝟙_) f`. NOTE: the `show` must apply `pushforward₀` to BOTH
   its args `(Over.forget X.unop) (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ …)`, else `rfl` mismatches.
4. `have key := naturality_apply φ (Over.homMk f.unop).op s` — used UNREWRITTEN. The
   `(restr X.unop M).map (Over.homMk f.unop).op` inside `key` is DEFEQ to `M.map f`
   (`restr_map_homMk`, `rfl`, cheap on abstract `M`), so the closing `Eq.trans` absorbs it.
   (`rw [restr_map_homMk M f] at key` FAILS to syntactically match — dropped; defeq does the work.)
5. `have hdt` (the `dual_map_app_terminal` content):
   `(((dual M).map f φ).app (op (Over.mk (𝟙 Y.unop)))).hom = (φ.app (op (Over.mk f.unop))).hom`,
   via `congrArg ModuleCat.Hom.hom (eq_of_heq (hom_app_heq φ (congrArg op (congrArg Over.mk
   (Category.id_comp f.unop)))))`. Lifted through `evalLin`'s defeq as
   `hdt2 := DFunLike.congr_fun hdt ((ConcreteCategory.hom (M.map f)) s)` (NOTE: `show …; rw [hdt]`
   FAILS on a `ModuleCat.Hom.hom`/`ConcreteCategory.hom` coercion-path mismatch — use
   `DFunLike.congr_fun`).
6. Assembly: `exact hdt2.trans (key.trans (star (evalLin M X φ s)))`. `Eq.trans` + `exact` close
   up to defeq (M-abstract side cheap; unit side stays symbolic), so the expensive
   `restr_map_homMk (𝟙_)` / unit `whnf` defeq is never triggered.

intro X Y f
    refine ModuleCat.MonoidalCategory.tensor_ext (fun s φ => ?_)
    rw [ModuleCat.hom_comp, ModuleCat.hom_comp, LinearMap.comp_apply, LinearMap.comp_apply]
    erw [PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj_map_tmul]
    rw [internalHomEvalApp_tmul, internalHomEvalApp_tmul]
    -- Goal G: evalLin M Y (dual.map f φ) (M.map f s) = ((𝟙_).map f).hom (evalLin M X φ s)
    erw [PresheafOfModules.unit_map_apply]
    -- Goal G': evalLin M Y (dual.map f φ) (M.map f s) = (R.map f) (evalLin M X φ s)
    -- (★): the restricted-unit transition map is the ring map (whnf-free, via pushforward lemmas).
    have star : ∀ r : (R₀.obj X : Type u),
        ((PresheafOfModules.restr X.unop
            (𝟙_ (PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)))).map
              (Over.homMk f.unop : Over.mk f.unop ⟶ Over.mk (𝟙 X.unop)).op).hom r
          = (ConcreteCategory.hom ((R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat).map f)) r := by
      intro r
      unfold PresheafOfModules.restr
      rw [show PresheafOfModules.pushforward₀ (Over.forget X.unop)
            = PresheafOfModules.pushforward
                (𝟙 ((Over.forget X.unop).op ⋙ R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)) from rfl]
      simp only [PresheafOfModules.pushforward_obj_map_apply, Over.forget_map, Over.homMk_left,
        PresheafOfModules.unit_map_apply]
    -- naturality of the dual section `φ` over the restricted site, for the morphism
    -- `(Over.homMk f.unop).op : op (Over.mk (𝟙 X.unop)) ⟶ op (Over.mk f.unop)`.
    have key := PresheafOfModules.naturality_apply
      (φ : PresheafOfModules.restr X.unop M ⟶ PresheafOfModules.restr X.unop
        (𝟙_ (PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat))))
      (Over.homMk f.unop : Over.mk f.unop ⟶ Over.mk (𝟙 X.unop)).op s
    rw [restr_map_homMk M f] at key
    -- `(dual.map f φ)` evaluated at the terminal section of `Over Y.unop` is `φ` evaluated at
    -- `op (Over.mk f.unop)`: the further-restriction reindexes the over-category along `Over.map`,
    -- and `Over.mk (𝟙 Y.unop ≫ f.unop) = Over.mk f.unop` by `id_comp` (whnf-free: M abstract).
    have hdt : (((dual M).map f φ).app (Opposite.op (Over.mk (𝟙 Y.unop)))).hom
             = (φ.app (Opposite.op (Over.mk f.unop))).hom := by
      apply congrArg ModuleCat.Hom.hom
      exact eq_of_heq (hom_app_heq φ

    refine ModuleCat.MonoidalCategory.tensor_ext (fun s φ => ?_)

              (Over.homMk f.unop : Over.mk f.unop ⟶ Over.mk (𝟙 X.unop)).op).hom r

      intro r

input.content:
# AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/TensorObjSubstrate.lean — iter-223

## Summary
- **Target:** close the `naturality` field of `PresheafOfModules.internalHomEval` (the iter-222
  typed `sorry`). **NOT closed.** sorry count: **4 → 4** (unchanged).
- **Build: GREEN.** Authoritative `lake env lean AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/TensorObjSubstrate.lean`
  → `EXIT=0`, errors=0; LSP `lean_diagnostic_messages severity=error` → `[]`. The 4 sorries are
  L634 `isLocallyInjective_whiskerLeft_of_W`, L1487 `internalHomEval` (this target), L1929
  `exists_tensorObj_inverse`, L1979 `addCommGroup_via_tensorObj`.
- **Verdict:** `Infrastructure missing / genuinely new obstacle` — the three planned routes
  (#1 generalize-unit, #2 pushforward-lemmas, #3 elementwise) ALL fail for the SAME deeper reason,
  newly characterized this iter by authoritative bisection. This is the explicit iter-224
  mathlib-analogist escalation trigger (progress-critic ts223 tripwire).

## THE OBSTACLE (newly characterized, authoritative `lake env lean` bisection)
The naturality goal, after `intro X Y f; refine ModuleCat.MonoidalCategory.tensor_ext (fun s φ => ?_)`,
is:
```
((tensorObj M (dual M)).map f ≫ (restrictScalars _).map (internalHomEvalApp M Y)).hom (s ⊗ₜ φ)
  = (internalHomEvalApp M X ≫ (𝟙_).map f).hom (s ⊗ₜ φ)
```
**EVERY goal-rewriting tactic bombs with `(deterministic) timeout at whnf, 200000 heartbeats` on its
FIRST rewrite** — confirmed by separate full compiles:
- `erw [ModuleCat.hom_comp, ModuleCat.hom_comp, LinearMap.comp_apply, LinearMap.comp_apply]` → BOMB
- `rw [PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorUnit_map]` → BOMB
- `rw [show (𝟙_ …) = PresheafOfModules.unit … from rfl]` → BOMB
- `simp only [PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorUnit_map]` → BOMB
- `attribute [local irreducible] internalHomEvalApp dual in …` + any of the above → STILL BOMB

**Root cause:** the goal's codomain is the `PresheafOfModules` monoidal unit `𝟙_`, and the whole
goal is saturated with `dual M = internalHom M 𝟙_` (via `s ⊗ₜ φ`'s type and the composition).
`kabstract` (used by `rw`/`erw`/`simp` to locate the rewrite site and build the motive) runs
`isDefEq` on these subterms, which forces a `whnf` of the monoidal-unit machinery whose normal form
is enormous (>200000 heartbeats, ~exponential — NOT budget-bound).

**Why this is DEEPER than the iter-222 diagnosis:** iter-222 localized the bomb to the single
`restr_map_homMk (𝟙_) f` step and proposed three routes that avoid *that* step. But the bomb
actually fires on the FIRST rewrite of ANY route — the unit toxicity is in the goal itself, not in
one lemma instantiation. iter-222's "verified in pieces" was via `lean_multi_attempt`, which does
NOT reproduce the full-elaboration heartbeat accounting; under real `lake` compile every route bombs.

**Why `local irreducible` doesn't help:** making the project defs `dual`/`internalHomEvalApp`
irreducible shields *their* bodies, but the toxic `whnf` is in *Mathlib's* `𝟙_` monoidal-unit
machinery (`PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorUnit` and its `.obj`/`.map`), which cannot be made
irreducible from the project. (Also: making `dual` irreducible breaks the `key` cast
`(φ : restr X.unop M ⟶ restr X.unop 𝟙_)`, which needs `(dual M).obj X` defeq the hom type.)

**`maxHeartbeats` is forbidden** by the objectives (cost is exponential, not budget-bound).

## The worked mathematics (correct; only the Lean assembly is blocked)
Preserved verbatim in a comment block above the `sorry` in-source. Six steps:
1. break the two `≫` on `s ⊗ₜ φ`, then `tensorObj_map_tmul` + `internalHomEvalApp_tmul ×2`
   ⇒ **G**: `evalLin M Y (dual.map f φ) (M.map f s) = ((𝟙_).map f).hom (evalLin M X φ s)`.
2. `PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorUnit_map` **[verified-exists iter-223]**:
   `(𝟙_).map f = ofHom (RingHom.toModule (R.map f))` → RHS becomes the ring map `R.map f`.
3. (★) `((restr X.unop 𝟙_).map (Over.homMk f.unop).op).hom r = (R.map f) r`, via
   `unfold restr; rw [show pushforward₀ (Over.forget X.unop) R = pushforward (𝟙 …) from rfl,
   pushforward_obj_map_apply, unit_map_apply]`.
4. `key := naturality_apply (φ : restr X.unop M ⟶ restr X.unop 𝟙_) (Over.homMk f.unop).op s`;
   its `(restr X.unop M).map _` side is DEFEQ to `M.map f` (`restr_map_homMk`, cheap on abstract `M`).
5. `hdt`: `((dual M).map f φ).app (op (Over.mk (𝟙 Y.unop))) = φ.app (op (Over.mk f.unop))` via
   `congrArg ModuleCat.Hom.hom (eq_of_heq (hom_app_heq φ (congrArg op (congrArg Over.mk
   (Category.id_comp f.unop)))))`; `hdt2 := DFunLike.congr_fun hdt _`.
6. close: `exact hdt2.trans (key.trans (star (evalLin M X φ s)))`.

Each step is individually sound; it is the *assembly against the live `𝟙_`-laden goal* (which
requires at least step-1/step-2 rewriting) that triggers the whnf bomb.

      𝟙_ (PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)) :=
  PresheafOfModules.Hom.mk (fun X => internalHomEvalApp M X) (by
    intro X Y f
    refine ModuleCat.MonoidalCategory.tensor_ext (fun s φ => ?_)
    rw [PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorUnit_map]

    erw [PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj_map_tmul]
    rw [internalHomEvalApp_tmul, internalHomEvalApp_tmul]
    -- Goal G: evalLin M Y (dual.map f φ) (M.map f s) = ((𝟙_).map f).hom (evalLin M X φ s)

    -- (whnf-free: `unit_map_apply` is a syntactic Mathlib simp lemma, no unit `whnf` forced).
    rw [show (𝟙_ (PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)))

    have star : ∀ r : (R₀.obj X : Type u),

            (𝟙_ (PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)))).map
              (Over.homMk f.unop : Over.mk f.unop ⟶ Over.mk (𝟙 X.unop)).op).hom r

      intro r
